observe: One block, one process. Clean, until the gaps between blocks start adding up.
fault: The space is there, technically. Just scattered into pieces too small to use. That's not nothing — that's fragmentation.
repair: Watch the partitions. See where the gaps are actually going.
escape: Don't let the gaps win. Keep the blocks usable.

observe: Contiguous memory allocation assigns each process one continuous block.
fault: External fragmentation is preventing allocation despite sufficient total free space.
repair: Identify the fragmented free space between allocated blocks.
escape: Confirm the fragmentation issue before time expires.

hints: Total free space and usable free space aren't the same thing when it's scattered.
